properties.text: encode_receipt MUST produce a receipt encoding whose first byte is exactly the type prefix corresponding to the transaction type: 0x01 for AccessListTransaction, 0x02 for FeeMarketTransaction, 0x03 for BlobTransaction, 0x04 for SetCodeTransaction. For LegacyTransaction, the receipt MUST be returned without any type prefix (raw RLP).

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit transactions of any supported type via P2P. Incorrect encoding causes consensus splits between clients that encode differently.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect receipt encoding produces a divergent receipts_root in the block header, causing a consensus split across the network. All clients must agree on encoding.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating that a specific transaction type produces a receipt encoding that differs from the canonical specification.

properties.text: encode_receipt MUST handle ALL transaction types exhaustively. If a new transaction type is introduced without updating encode_receipt, the function MUST NOT silently fall through to the legacy encoding path. The set {AccessListTransaction, FeeMarketTransaction, BlobTransaction, SetCodeTransaction, LegacyTransaction} MUST be exhaustive.

reachability.notes: If a future transaction type is added without updating encode_receipt, receipts for that type would be silently encoded as legacy, producing incorrect encodings that cause consensus splits.

properties.severity_justification: Silent fall-through to legacy encoding for an unrecognized transaction type corrupts the receipts_root and causes consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstration of a transaction type that is not handled by the type-dispatch in encode_receipt.

properties.text: LegacyTransaction receipts MUST be returned as raw Receipt objects without RLP wrapping or type-prefix prepending. forall (tx : LegacyTransaction, receipt : Receipt): encode_receipt(tx, receipt) == receipt.

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit legacy transactions via P2P. Wrapping a legacy receipt would alter the receipts_root.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect encoding of legacy receipts would cause receipt root mismatch and block rejection or consensus split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a legacy transaction and demonstrate receipt encoding differs from raw Receipt object.

properties.text: decode_receipt MUST assert that if the receipt is of type Bytes, the first byte MUST be in the set {1, 2, 3, 4}. Any other first byte value MUST cause an assertion failure. forall (receipt : Bytes): receipt[0] not in {1, 2, 3, 4} => decode_receipt(receipt) reverts.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could send a receipt with an invalid type-prefix byte via devp2p. The assertion is the primary gatekeeper.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the type-prefix assertion could cause the decoder to accept invalid receipts, leading to state corruption or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a receipt with type-prefix byte outside {1,2,3,4} and demonstrate it passes decode_receipt without assertion failure.

properties.text: decode_receipt MUST strip exactly the first byte (type prefix) before RLP decoding the remainder. forall (receipt : Bytes, receipt[0] in {1,2,3,4}): decode_receipt(receipt) == rlp.decode_to(Receipt, receipt[1:]).

reachability.notes: Incorrect offset in byte stripping would cause RLP decoding to fail or produce corrupt receipt data.

properties.severity_justification: Off-by-one in prefix stripping corrupts all receipt decoding, causing consensus splits between clients that decode differently.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that decode_receipt strips an incorrect number of bytes before RLP decoding.

properties.text: decode_receipt: If the receipt is not of type Bytes (i.e., it is already a Receipt object), it MUST be returned unchanged. forall (receipt : Receipt, not isinstance(receipt, Bytes)): decode_receipt(receipt) == receipt.

reachability.notes: This path is only triggered when receipt is already decoded (internal processing). Not directly reachable via external input.

properties.severity_justification: Impacts internal correctness but requires a pre-decoded receipt object to trigger.

properties.text: encode_receipt and decode_receipt MUST be inverse operations for all typed transactions: forall (tx, receipt): decode_receipt(encode_receipt(tx, receipt)) == receipt.

reachability.notes: If encode and decode are not exact inverses, data transmitted between peers via P2P will be corrupted, causing network partitions.

properties.severity_justification: Non-invertible encoding/decoding causes data corruption across the P2P network and consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a receipt where encode followed by decode produces a different receipt object.

properties.text: The Withdrawal data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). All four fields (index: U64, validator_index: U64, address: Address, amount: U256) MUST be set before the freeze operation.

reachability.notes: Withdrawals originate from the consensus layer via Engine API. A compromised CL could send malformed withdrawal instructions.

properties.severity_justification: Mutable withdrawal objects could be tampered with after construction, leading to unauthorized ETH credits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate mutation of a Withdrawal object after freeze, showing unauthorized balance modification.

properties.text: All Withdrawal fields MUST be set during construction. A Withdrawal with any unset field MUST NOT be freezable. forall (w : Withdrawal): freeze(w) requires w.index != None AND w.validator_index != None AND w.address != None AND w.amount != None.

reachability.notes: Partial withdrawal construction could lead to undefined behavior when serialized or processed.

properties.severity_justification: Partially constructed withdrawals could cause undefined behavior or crashes during serialization.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Withdrawal with a missing field can be frozen and used in block processing.

properties.text: The Header data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). All 21 fields MUST be set in the specified construction order before freeze.

reachability.notes: Block headers arrive via P2P from peers. A mutable header could be tampered with after initial validation.

properties.severity_justification: A mutable header object could be modified after validation, bypassing consensus checks and allowing invalid state transitions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Header field can be mutated after freeze, allowing post-validation tampering.

properties.text: In post-merge (PoS) forks, the Header difficulty field MUST be 0 and the nonce field MUST be zero-bytes. forall (header : Header, post_merge): header.difficulty == 0 AND header.nonce == Bytes8(0).

reachability.notes: A peer could transmit a block with non-zero difficulty or nonce in post-merge context, violating PoS constraints.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting a block with non-zero difficulty/nonce in PoS could indicate a consensus rule violation, allowing invalid blocks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a post-merge block with non-zero difficulty and demonstrate it passes header validation.

properties.text: In post-merge forks, the ommers_hash in the Header MUST always equal EMPTY_OMMER_HASH. forall (header : Header, post_merge): header.ommers_hash == EMPTY_OMMER_HASH.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could submit a block with non-empty ommers_hash to exploit clients that don't validate this field.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting blocks with non-empty ommers in PoS would break protocol invariants and could enable uncle reward exploitation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a post-merge block with ommers_hash != EMPTY_OMMER_HASH and show it passes validation.

properties.text: The base_fee_per_gas in the Header MUST follow EIP-1559 dynamic fee adjustment rules relative to the parent block's base fee and gas usage.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could set an incorrect base_fee_per_gas to undercharge for block space.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect base fee allows blocks with mispriced transactions, undermining the EIP-1559 fee market and potentially enabling spam attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with incorrect base_fee_per_gas that passes validation.

properties.text: The blob_gas_used and excess_blob_gas fields in the Header MUST follow EIP-4844 blob gas accounting rules.

reachability.notes: Incorrect blob gas accounting allows blob spam or underpriced blob transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect blob gas accounting breaks the EIP-4844 fee market for data availability and could enable underpriced blob spam.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with incorrect blob_gas_used or excess_blob_gas that passes validation.

properties.text: The requests_hash field in the Header MUST follow EIP-7685 general purpose request computation. forall (header): header.requests_hash == compute_requests_hash(requests).

reachability.notes: Incorrect requests_hash causes CL-EL disagreement and block rejection at consensus level.

properties.severity_justification: requests_hash mismatch between CL and EL causes consensus failure, preventing block finalization.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where requests_hash does not match the computed value from the actual requests.

properties.text: The Block data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Block consists of (header: Header, transactions: Tuple[Bytes|LegacyTransaction, ...], ommers: Tuple[Header, ...], withdrawals: Tuple[Withdrawal, ...]).

reachability.notes: Blocks arrive via P2P. Mutability after construction could allow post-validation tampering.

properties.severity_justification: Mutable block objects could be tampered with between validation and execution, allowing invalid state transitions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Block object can be mutated after construction and freeze.

properties.text: In post-merge (PoS) forks, the ommers tuple in a Block MUST always be empty. forall (block : Block, post_merge): len(block.ommers) == 0.

reachability.notes: A peer could transmit a block with non-empty ommers to exploit clients that don't validate this post-merge.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting blocks with ommers in PoS violates protocol invariants and could enable reward manipulation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a post-merge block with non-empty ommers and demonstrate it passes validation.

properties.text: Block transactions may contain any supported transaction type (Legacy, AccessList, FeeMarket, Blob, SetCode). The transactions field MUST accept Tuple[Bytes | LegacyTransaction, ...].

reachability.notes: All transaction types must be correctly handled. Missing support for a type causes block rejection.

properties.severity_justification: Failure to support a valid transaction type causes nodes to reject valid blocks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a valid transaction of each type and demonstrate block construction rejects any valid type.

properties.text: The Log data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Log consists of (address: Address, topics: Tuple[Hash32, ...], data: Bytes).

reachability.notes: Logs are produced during transaction execution. Mutable logs could be tampered with before receipt construction.

properties.severity_justification: Mutable logs could be altered between emission and receipt construction, corrupting bloom filters and receipts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Log object can be mutated after freeze.

properties.text: Log topics tuple MUST have at most 4 elements, corresponding to LOG0 through LOG4 opcodes. forall (log : Log): len(log.topics) <= 4.

reachability.notes: The EVM enforces the LOG0-LOG4 opcode limit. A bug in the EVM allowing > 4 topics would corrupt bloom filters.

properties.severity_justification: Exceeding the topic limit would cause bloom filter inconsistencies and receipt validation failures.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log with > 4 topics being emitted and processed without rejection.

properties.text: The Receipt data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Receipt consists of (succeeded: bool, cumulative_gas_used: Uint, bloom: Bloom, logs: Tuple[Log, ...]).

reachability.notes: Receipts are constructed during block processing and transmitted via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Mutable receipts could be tampered with after construction, corrupting the receipts trie and block validation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Receipt object can be mutated after freeze.

properties.text: The cumulative_gas_used field in Receipt MUST be monotonically non-decreasing within a block. forall (block, i < j): block.receipts[i].cumulative_gas_used <= block.receipts[j].cumulative_gas_used.

reachability.notes: A malicious block builder could order transactions such that cumulative gas decreases, indicating a gas accounting bug.

properties.severity_justification: Non-monotonic cumulative gas indicates a gas accounting vulnerability that could allow gas undercharging.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where cumulative_gas_used decreases between consecutive receipts.

properties.text: The bloom filter in a Receipt MUST be consistent with the logs in that receipt. forall (receipt): receipt.bloom == logs_bloom(receipt.logs).

reachability.notes: An inconsistent bloom filter causes false negatives in log queries, affecting dApps and indexers.

properties.severity_justification: Bloom filter inconsistency causes silent failure in log filtering, breaking all dApp event subscriptions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a receipt where bloom does not match logs_bloom(receipt.logs).

properties.text: The bloom bytearray MUST be exactly 256 bytes (2048 bits). forall (bloom): len(bloom) == 256.

reachability.notes: An under- or over-sized bloom array would cause index-out-of-bounds errors or incorrect bit-setting.

properties.severity_justification: Wrong bloom filter size causes out-of-bounds access (crash) or incorrect bloom (consensus split).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: trigger a bloom filter operation with a non-256-byte array and demonstrate crash or incorrect behavior.

properties.text: In add_to_bloom, the bit_to_set value MUST always be in range [0, 2047] due to the 11-bit mask 0x07FF. forall (hashed, idx): Uint.from_be_bytes(hashed[idx:idx+2]) AND 0x07FF is in [0, 2047].

reachability.notes: If the masking is incorrect, bit indices could exceed the 2048-bit bloom filter, causing OOB writes.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-range bit indices cause memory corruption or consensus-divergent bloom filters.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a bloom entry that produces a bit_to_set outside [0, 2047].

properties.text: In add_to_bloom, the byte_index MUST always be in range [0, 255]. forall computation: byte_index = bit_index // 8 where bit_index is in [0, 2047], thus byte_index is in [0, 255].

reachability.notes: Derived from bit_to_set invariant. If bit_to_set is correct, byte_index is automatically bounded.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-range byte index causes array out-of-bounds write, crashing the node.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a byte_index > 255 being computed, causing an out-of-bounds access.

properties.text: The add_to_bloom loop MUST iterate exactly 3 times (at indices 0, 2, 4), setting 3 bits per bloom entry. No more, no fewer iterations are permitted.

reachability.notes: Fewer or more iterations produce a bloom filter incompatible with the spec, causing consensus splits.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect number of bit-settings per bloom entry produces a bloom filter that diverges across clients, causing consensus split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that add_to_bloom sets a number of bits != 3 for any input.

properties.text: The keccak256 output used in add_to_bloom MUST be at least 6 bytes to support 3 pairs of 2-byte reads at indices (0,1), (2,3), (4,5).

reachability.notes: keccak256 always produces 32 bytes. This invariant holds by construction of the hash function.

properties.severity_justification: keccak256 always produces 32 bytes (> 6). This invariant is satisfied by the hash function specification.

properties.text: logs_bloom MUST initialize the bloom bytearray to all zeros (256 bytes) before processing any logs. forall: at the start of logs_bloom, bloom == bytearray(256 * [0]).

reachability.notes: If the bloom is not zeroed, residual bits from previous computations could cause false positives.

properties.severity_justification: Non-zeroed bloom initialization causes incorrect bloom filters, leading to consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that logs_bloom reuses a non-zeroed buffer, producing an incorrect bloom.

properties.text: logs_bloom MUST add every log address to the bloom filter exactly once per log. forall (log in logs): add_to_bloom(bloom, log.address) is called exactly once.

reachability.notes: Missing or duplicate address additions produce an incorrect bloom filter.

properties.severity_justification: Missing an address from the bloom causes false negatives in log filtering.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log address that is not added to the bloom filter.

properties.text: logs_bloom MUST add every topic of every log to the bloom filter exactly once. forall (log in logs, topic in log.topics): add_to_bloom(bloom, topic) is called exactly once.

reachability.notes: Missing topic additions produce incorrect bloom filters, causing false negatives.

properties.severity_justification: Missing a topic from the bloom causes false negatives in topic-based log filtering.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a log topic that is not added to the bloom filter.

properties.text: logs_bloom MUST return the final Bloom object only after ALL logs have been processed. The bloom MUST NOT be returned prematurely (before processing all logs).

reachability.notes: Early return would produce an incomplete bloom filter missing entries from unprocessed logs.

properties.severity_justification: Incomplete bloom filter causes silent false negatives in log queries.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that logs_bloom returns before processing all logs in the input.

properties.text: add_to_bloom MUST be called exactly (1 + len(log.topics)) times per log entry: once for the address and once for each topic. forall (log): calls_to_add_to_bloom(log) == 1 + len(log.topics).

reachability.notes: Incorrect call count produces a bloom that is either incomplete or has extra entries.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect bloom call count produces a non-standard bloom filter, causing consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate add_to_bloom is called an incorrect number of times for a given log.

properties.text: decode_receipt MUST reject receipts received from P2P peers with type-prefix bytes outside {1, 2, 3, 4} via assertion failure. Specifically: assert receipt[0] in (1, 2, 3, 4).

reachability.notes: A malicious peer can send crafted receipt bytes via devp2p with arbitrary first byte.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the assertion allows the decoder to process arbitrary byte sequences as receipts, potentially causing memory corruption or state confusion.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a receipt payload with an invalid type prefix that is accepted by decode_receipt.

properties.text: A peer MUST NOT be able to send a receipt encoded with a type prefix different from the actual transaction type. Receipt type prefixes MUST be verified against the corresponding transaction type during block validation.

reachability.notes: Type prefix mismatch could cause the execution engine to misattribute receipts to wrong transaction types.

properties.severity_justification: Misattributing receipt types corrupts the receipts_root and causes consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: send a receipt with type prefix 0x02 for a BlobTransaction and show it is accepted.

properties.text: decode_receipt MUST safely handle extremely large RLP-encoded receipts or deeply nested structures from P2P peers without crashing or consuming excessive memory.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer can send oversized or deeply nested RLP payloads to exhaust memory.

properties.severity_justification: Memory exhaustion or CPU exhaustion via crafted RLP payloads causes node crash (DoS).
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: send a crafted RLP payload that causes decode_receipt to consume excessive memory or CPU.

properties.text: A bug in the RLP codec MUST NOT produce non-canonical encodings for Receipt objects. All client implementations MUST produce bit-identical RLP output for the same Receipt input. forall (receipt, client_A, client_B): rlp.encode(receipt, client_A) == rlp.encode(receipt, client_B).

reachability.notes: Cross-client RLP encoding differences in receipts cause receipts_root divergence and consensus splits.

properties.severity_justification: Non-canonical RLP encoding directly causes consensus splits between clients using different RLP implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a Receipt that encodes to different bytes in two different client implementations.

properties.text: The keccak256 implementation used in bloom filter construction (add_to_bloom) MUST produce correct hash outputs. Incorrect hashes would set bloom bits at wrong positions, causing false negatives in log search.

reachability.notes: All log emissions trigger bloom filter computation via keccak256. Incorrect hashes produce divergent blooms.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect keccak256 in bloom computation causes bloom filter divergence across clients, resulting in consensus split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a keccak256 output that differs from the reference implementation for a specific input.

properties.text: encode_receipt's type-dispatch logic MUST NOT silently encode receipts for unrecognized transaction types as legacy receipts. The isinstance fall-through path MUST only be reached for genuine LegacyTransaction instances.

reachability.notes: Silent fall-through for future transaction types would produce incorrect receipt encodings.

properties.severity_justification: Silent misencoding of receipts for new transaction types causes consensus failures that are extremely difficult to debug.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: introduce a mock transaction type not in the isinstance chain and show encode_receipt produces legacy encoding.

properties.text: A malicious block proposer MUST NOT be able to set a future timestamp for MEV advantages. The header timestamp MUST be strictly greater than the parent block's timestamp. forall (header, parent): header.timestamp > parent.timestamp.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could manipulate the timestamp field within the block header.

properties.severity_justification: Timestamp manipulation enables MEV extraction and disrupts block ordering invariants.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with timestamp <= parent.timestamp that passes header validation.

properties.text: A proposer MUST NOT be able to construct a block that inflates bloom filter and receipt processing overhead beyond gas-bounded limits. Log emission is bounded by the block gas limit and LOG opcode gas costs.

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit many log-heavy transactions to maximize bloom filter processing time.

properties.severity_justification: Excessive log processing slows block validation but is bounded by gas limits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block that causes disproportionate bloom filter processing time relative to gas consumed.

properties.text: Block contents MUST be non-repudiable: the block header's parent_hash, state_root, transactions_root, receipt_root, and withdrawals_root commit to the full block contents via Merkle Patricia Trie roots. The consensus layer signs blocks via BLS signatures, binding proposer identity to the block.

reachability.notes: All block contents are committed via cryptographic roots in the header.

properties.severity_justification: Repudiation resistance is important for accountability but does not directly enable attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block whose committed roots do not match the actual block contents.

properties.text: Corrupted withdrawal instructions from the consensus layer MUST NOT credit incorrect amounts to wrong addresses. The withdrawals_root in the header MUST commit to the exact withdrawal list.

reachability.notes: The Engine API is authenticated via JWT, but a compromised CL could inject invalid withdrawals.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect withdrawal processing causes unauthorized ETH minting, resulting in fund loss across the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that corrupted withdrawal data passes validation and credits incorrect amounts.

properties.text: An attacker at the consensus-execution interface MUST NOT be able to inject fabricated withdrawal instructions not authorized by the beacon chain. The Engine API MUST use JWT authentication and the parent_beacon_block_root MUST provide a cryptographic anchor.

reachability.notes: Engine API is authenticated but an attacker with JWT access could spoof withdrawals.

properties.severity_justification: Unauthorized ETH minting via fabricated withdrawals is a critical fund loss vulnerability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate injection of unauthorized withdrawals via the Engine API.

properties.text: The bloom filter (logs_bloom) MUST NOT reveal information beyond what is already publicly available in transaction logs. The bloom filter is an accepted design trade-off for compact log indexing.

reachability.notes: Bloom filter information disclosure is by design. Addresses and topics are already public on-chain.

properties.severity_justification: The bloom filter does not leak additional private information beyond full receipt data. This is a design trade-off.

properties.text: A contract MUST NOT be able to emit excessive logs that cause bloom filter construction to process a number of add_to_bloom calls that exceeds what the block gas limit permits. forall (block): total_add_to_bloom_calls(block) <= gas_bounded_maximum.

reachability.notes: Each LOG opcode charges gas proportional to topics and data size, naturally limiting total logs per block.

properties.severity_justification: Excessive log processing is bounded by gas limits but could still cause disproportionate processing time.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where bloom filter processing time is disproportionate to gas consumed.

properties.text: Encoded receipts transmitted to peers via P2P MUST NOT reveal information beyond what is already public by design (transaction success/failure, cumulative gas, bloom filter, logs). Receipt data is public in Ethereum's transparency model.

reachability.notes: Receipt transparency is by design. The devp2p protocol does not provide confidentiality.

properties.severity_justification: Receipt data is inherently public. No confidentiality is expected or provided.

properties.text: A transaction's fields (gas, value, data, to, nonce) MUST NOT be modifiable in transit between the sender and the execution engine. The ECDSA signature covers the full transaction payload and any modification invalidates the signature.

reachability.notes: Transaction integrity is enforced by ECDSA signatures. Any modification invalidates the signature.

properties.severity_justification: Transaction field modification would allow unauthorized fund transfers or contract interactions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a modified transaction that passes ECDSA signature verification.

properties.text: SetCodeTransaction (EIP-7702) authorizations MUST be individually signed by the authorizing account. The execution engine MUST validate each authorization signature before granting code delegation privileges.

reachability.notes: EIP-7702 allows code delegation for EOAs. Weak authorization validation enables account takeover.

properties.severity_justification: Unauthorized code delegation allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code under the victim's account identity, enabling full fund theft.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate an EIP-7702 authorization with a forged signature that is accepted by the execution engine.

properties.text: RLP encoding of accounts via encode_account MUST maintain the canonical tuple ordering (nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash). Any deviation from this ordering causes consensus incompatibility.

reachability.notes: Account encoding affects the state root. Non-canonical encoding produces a divergent state root.

properties.severity_justification: Non-canonical account RLP encoding produces a different state_root, causing a network-wide consensus split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate an account encoding with incorrect field ordering that produces a valid-looking but divergent state root.

properties.text: The deposit contract address check in parse_deposit_requests MUST use exact equality: log.address == DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS. No other contract address MUST be accepted for deposit event parsing.

reachability.notes: A malicious contract could emit logs mimicking the deposit event signature at a different address.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting deposit events from non-canonical addresses allows injection of fraudulent deposits into the consensus layer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: deploy a contract that emits deposit-like events and demonstrate they are accepted by parse_deposit_requests.

properties.text: extract_deposit_data MUST validate the total data length to be exactly 576 bytes and all five field offsets (PUBKEY_OFFSET=160, WC_OFFSET=256, AMOUNT_OFFSET=320, SIGNATURE_OFFSET=384, INDEX_OFFSET=512) and sizes (48, 32, 8, 96, 8) against expected constants. Any deviation MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: A compromised deposit contract could emit malformed data. The extraction function must validate rigorously.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting malformed deposit data corrupts the deposit request stream and could crash the consensus layer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: emit a deposit event with data length != 576 or incorrect offsets and show it passes extract_deposit_data.

properties.text: compute_requests_hash MUST produce identical output for identical input across all execution client implementations. The SHA-256 two-level construction (hash each request individually, then feed digests into outer hash) MUST be deterministic.

reachability.notes: Different hash outputs across clients cause block rejection at the CL, preventing finalization.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic requests_hash causes CL-EL disagreement and prevents block finalization across the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that two client implementations produce different requests_hash for identical input.

properties.text: EIP-7702 Authorization MUST include chain_id for cross-chain replay protection and nonce for same-chain replay protection. An authorization replayed on a different chain or with a stale nonce MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: An attacker can capture a valid authorization and replay it on another chain or after a nonce increment.

properties.severity_justification: Authorization replay enables unauthorized code delegation, leading to full account compromise and fund theft.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: capture a valid EIP-7702 authorization and replay it on a different chain or after nonce changes.

properties.text: The storage_root parameter passed to encode_account MUST be the correct 32-byte Merkle root of the account's storage trie. encode_account DOES NOT validate this value; the caller MUST provide the correct root.

reachability.notes: The caller must ensure storage_root correctness. An internal bug could provide an incorrect root.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect storage_root corrupts the state trie and causes consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires an internal caller to provide an incorrect storage_root. Not directly externally reachable.

properties.text: EMPTY_ACCOUNT MUST be an immutable module-level frozen constant that cannot be mutated after construction. forall: mutation_attempt(EMPTY_ACCOUNT) raises error.

reachability.notes: Immutability is enforced by Python's frozen dataclass mechanism.

properties.severity_justification: EMPTY_ACCOUNT mutation would require bypassing Python's frozen dataclass mechanism.

properties.text: Type aliases (Address=Bytes20, Root=Hash32, VersionedHash=Hash32, Bloom=Bytes256) MUST enforce byte-length constraints at construction time. forall (addr : Address): len(addr) == 20; forall (root : Root): len(root) == 32; forall (bloom : Bloom): len(bloom) == 256.

reachability.notes: If type alias length enforcement is weak, malformed data could propagate through the system.

properties.severity_justification: Weak type enforcement allows malformed data to propagate, causing downstream errors or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct an Address with len != 20 and demonstrate it is accepted by the type system.

properties.text: Receipt processing order (block_output.receipt_keys) MUST deterministically define the deposit request ordering. All compliant execution clients MUST iterate receipt keys in the same order to produce identical deposit_requests bytes.

reachability.notes: A malicious block builder controls receipt_keys ordering. Non-deterministic ordering causes requests_hash divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic deposit ordering causes requests_hash mismatch, preventing block finalization.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate two clients producing different deposit request orderings for the same block.

properties.text: trie_get MUST always return a valid (non-None) receipt for every key in block_output.receipt_keys. A None return MUST trigger an assertion failure, halting execution rather than silently skipping deposits.

reachability.notes: A crafted block with invalid receipt keys could trigger the assertion, crashing the client.

properties.severity_justification: A crafted block that triggers the assertion failure crashes the execution client, causing a DoS.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a block where a receipt key has no corresponding entry in the receipts trie and demonstrate client crash.

properties.text: A transaction sender MUST NOT be able to impersonate a different sender. The recover_sender function MUST use SECP256K1 signature recovery to derive the sender address from the transaction signature. Only the holder of the private key can produce a valid signature.

reachability.notes: Transaction sender authentication is fundamental to Ethereum security.

properties.severity_justification: Sender impersonation enables unauthorized fund transfers and contract interactions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a transaction with a forged signature that recovers to a different sender address.
